channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How contagious is Ebola? And how worried should you be about the current outbreak?
item.description: This outbreak is being called "the perfect storm." How did it start, what are the characteristics of the strain that's causing it and how much of a threat is it to global health?
item.content:encoded: This outbreak is being called "the perfect storm." How did it start, what are the characteristics of the strain that's causing it and how much of a threat is it to global health?
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Barney Frank, former congressman and gay-rights pioneer, dies at 86
item.description: The former congressman was politically engaged until the end. As GBH reports, Even in hospice, Frank was actively working to spread the message of his forthcoming book, "The Hard Path to Unity: Why We Must Reform the Left to Rescue Democracy."
item.content:encoded:
The former congressman was politically engaged until the end. As GBH reports, Even in hospice, Frank was actively working to spread the message of his forthcoming book, "The Hard Path to Unity: Why We Must Reform the Left to Rescue Democracy."

(Image credit: KAREN BLEIER)
item.dc:creator: The NPR Network

item.title: How one Oregon city has raised a billion dollars for climate change
item.description: The Portland Clean Energy Fund has raised a billion dollars for climate change action via a retail tax on large corporations in the city. Other cities are starting to shape similar funds.
item.content:encoded:
The Portland Clean Energy Fund has raised a billion dollars for climate change action via a retail tax on large corporations in the city. Other cities are starting to shape similar funds.

(Image credit: Monica Samayoa)
item.dc:creator: Monica Samayoa

item.title: Takeaways from Tuesday's primaries. And, victims of mosque shooting revealed
item.description: Trump-backed Ed Gallrein ousted Congressman Thomas Massie in Kentucky's Republican House primary. And, police have identified the victims in California's mosque shooting.
item.content:encoded:
Trump-backed Ed Gallrein ousted Congressman Thomas Massie in Kentucky's Republican House primary. And, police have identified the victims in California's mosque shooting.

(Image credit: Jon Cherry)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Former Spanish Prime Minister Zapatero is under investigation
item.description: A Spanish court is investigating former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero for alleged influence peddling and other possible crimes tied to a government airline bailout.
item.content:encoded:
A Spanish court is investigating former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero for alleged influence peddling and other possible crimes tied to a government airline bailout.

(Image credit: Juan Karita)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Gun rights groups see 'golden age' under Trump administration
item.description: New gun rules and lawsuits against states signal how this Trump administration is using its powers to align itself with the wishes of the gun rights movement.
item.content:encoded:
New gun rules and lawsuits against states signal how this Trump administration is using its powers to align itself with the wishes of the gun rights movement.

(Image credit: Tyler Kaufman)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Denver has a plan to heat and cool buildings without fossil fuels. It involves … sewage?
item.description: Like many cities, Denver's largest source of greenhouse gas emissions is its buildings. Heating and cooling skyscrapers requires a lot of fossil fuels. Now, the city is trying a surprising solution.
item.content:encoded:
Like many cities, Denver's largest source of greenhouse gas emissions is its buildings. Heating and cooling skyscrapers requires a lot of fossil fuels. Now, the city is trying a surprising solution.

(Image credit: Aaron Ontiveroz)
item.dc:creator: Ishan Thakore

item.title: Bees have coexisted with us for over a millennium. Their name remains a mystery
item.description: Its name is short — like its size — but the bee is one of Earth's most important and busy creatures.
item.content:encoded:
Its name is short — like its size — but the bee is one of Earth's most important and busy creatures.

(Image credit: Alan Nakkash for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Overworked and understaffed: Special ed teachers turn to AI for help
item.description: A fast-growing number of special educators nationwide are using AI to create customized education plans. Despite the risks, some research shows it could improve the quality of teachers' work.
item.content:encoded:
A fast-growing number of special educators nationwide are using AI to create customized education plans. Despite the risks, some research shows it could improve the quality of teachers' work.

(Image credit: Talia Herman for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: 4 takeaways from Tuesday's primary night in half a dozen states
item.description: While Trump continues to rack up victories on his vengeance tour, general election opponents are waiting in swing districts and swing states. Can front-line GOP candidates navigate these choppy waters?
item.content:encoded:
While Trump continues to rack up victories on his vengeance tour, general election opponents are waiting in swing districts and swing states. Can front-line GOP candidates navigate these choppy waters?

(Image credit: Brynn Anderson)
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro
